name: Gbarpolu County (Liberia)
description: Golden-yellow field with the Liberian national flag as a canton in the upper hoist; a white diamond shape stands at the centre of the field and a green flowering plant fills the lower-fly area.
history: Gbarpolu was created in 2001 from territory taken from Lofa County; this flag follows the design pattern of the original counties' flags presented by President Tubman in 1965.

features.symbolism: Gbarpolu as part of the Republic of Liberia
the diamond mining for which Gbarpolu is known
the agricultural produce and forest flora of the county
